theory.canonical_statement: The five-dimensional metric is decomposed into a four-dimensional metric and a compact coordinate whose off-diagonal term carries the electromagnetic potential.
theory.technical_interpretation: The source states that the extra coordinate y is compactified and that A_mu emerges as an electromagnetic potential from the higher-dimensional metric.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: The source-backed metric separates four-dimensional geometry from the compact-direction contribution.
The off-diagonal metric content is interpreted as the gauge potential associated with the compact coordinate.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\paragraph{Primary Equation.}
\[
ds^2_{(5)} = g^{(4)}_{\mu\nu}\,dx^{\mu}dx^{\nu} + \phi^2\!\left(dy + A_{\mu}dx^{\mu}\right)^2.
\]
\paragraph{Source-Grounded Reading.}
The compact coordinate $y$ enters only through the one-form $dy+A_{\mu}dx^{\mu}$. Expanding its square exhibits the cross term $2\phi^2 A_{\mu}\,dy\,dx^{\mu}$, so the vector potential is carried by the mixed compact--four-dimensional metric component.
\[
\phi^2\left(dy+A_{\mu}dx^{\mu}\right)^2=\phi^2dy^2+2\phi^2A_{\mu}\,dy\,dx^{\mu}+\phi^2A_{\mu}A_{\nu}\,dx^{\mu}dx^{\nu}.
\]
\paragraph{Interpretation.}
This repairs the entry as a Kaluza--Klein metric decomposition; it does not reuse the unrelated metric-degeneracy derivation previously attached to ID 0204.
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER

source_repair_review.basis: The extracted source explicitly states the corrected five-dimensional metric ansatz, compact y coordinate, and A_mu gauge-potential role.

review_source_enrichment.method: equation-aligned PDF evidence followed by manual review draft

symbol_definitions.ds^2_{(5)}: five-dimensional metric interval in the compactified ansatz
symbol_definitions.\\phi: scalar field scaling the compact-direction contribution
symbol_definitions.A_{\\mu}: electromagnetic potential emerging from the higher-dimensional metric

dictionary_sections.definition: The Kaluza--Klein metric ansatz is a five-dimensional geometric decomposition in which a four-dimensional spacetime metric is augmented by one compact coordinate. In the sourced form, the compact contribution is carried by the one-form \(dy+A_{\mu}dx^{\mu}\).
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: The entry organizes gravitation and gauge structure inside a single higher-dimensional metric description. The source identifies \(y\) as compactified and states that \(A_{\mu}\) emerges as an electromagnetic potential, so the gauge field is not appended as unrelated data but read from the mixed metric content.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: Expanding the compact term produces \(\phi^2dy^2+2\phi^2A_{\mu}\,dy\,dx^{\mu}+\phi^2A_{\mu}A_{\nu}\,dx^{\mu}dx^{\nu}\). The cross term supplies the explicit mathematical location of the geometric gauge contribution.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The retrieved source gives the five-dimensional metric formula, names the extra coordinate \(y\) as compactified, and identifies \(A_{\mu}\) as the emerging electromagnetic potential. It also describes the gauge potential as arising from off-diagonal metric components.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: This is a decomposition rule for the modeled geometry. It establishes the formal placement of the compact scalar and gauge potential in the metric; it does not by itself demonstrate a measured extra dimension or a realized electromagnetic unification.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: \(ds^2_{(5)}\) is the five-dimensional interval; \(g^{(4)}_{\mu\nu}\) is the four-dimensional metric sector; \(y\) is the compact coordinate; \(A_{\mu}\) is the electromagnetic-potential term; and \(\phi\) scales the compact-direction contribution.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry introduces the compact geometric carrier needed by later mode and resonance entries: the extra-dimensional sector is made mathematically explicit before its indexed excitations are compared with acoustic modes.

tzp_thesaurus.meaning: The additional coordinate identified by the source as compactified.
The vector-potential contribution read from mixed higher-dimensional metric components.
An assumed geometric form that fixes the fields and sectors being modeled.

review_publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: The compact one-form expands into a mixed \(dy\,dx^{\mu}\) contribution, formally locating \(A_{\mu}\) in the off-diagonal part of the five-dimensional metric.
review_publication_overrides.proof_latex:
\textbf{Statement:} The sourced metric ansatz is
\[
ds^2_{(5)}=g^{(4)}_{\mu\nu}\,dx^{\mu}dx^{\nu}+\phi^2\!\left(dy+A_{\mu}dx^{\mu}\right)^2.
\]
\textbf{Logic Validation:}\\
\textbf{Proposition:} The gauge-potential contribution occurs in a compact--four-dimensional mixed metric term.\\
\textbf{Proof:} Algebraic expansion of the square gives \(\phi^2dy^2+2\phi^2A_{\mu}\,dy\,dx^{\mu}+\phi^2A_{\mu}A_{\nu}\,dx^{\mu}dx^{\nu}\). Hence the mixed term is proportional to \(A_{\mu}\).\\
\textbf{VERDICT:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ \textbf{TRUE} --- formal consequence of the sourced ansatz\\
\textbf{Formal Status:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ THEORETICAL -- source-backed CHL obligation\\
\textbf{Physical Status:} THEORETICAL -- compact-dimension realization remains model-dependent
